query: AuRA complaints OR frustrating OR broken

results.title: Issues with the aura - Community Support - Glowforge Owners Forum
results.content:
If you could provide us with more detailed information, we might be able to help you more. There are an incredible group of folks in here with vast knowledge of lots of stuff.  
Without seeing any photos or reading any specifics, my first suggestion would be to try a project first using something like cardboard that won’t be an expensive loss in material.

I have an Aura…and have never yet had any issues…so it’s not a wide-spread problem. If you have orders to fill, you must have been able at some time to produce some things.

Please take heart and give us more information so that we might be able to help you get up and running again.

yes post specific problems. it is impossible that you are experiencing something nobody else in hasn’t already dealt with. [...] I have have my glowforge a little over 2 months now and the amount of time I spend trying to get it to work or troubleshooting it is very disappointing… I spent 1200 plus dollars on a machine I cant even use half the time and I am stuck with it because of their return policy. The amount of materals that are ruined is ridiculous, the amount of frustration and tears I have had over this thing is more than I have ever had over any item I have owned. There is ZERO customer support and every time I try to get help I get the same responses from what seems like an automated response system. I am very disappointed that someone would charge this much for a machine and hype it up just to find out it barely works, and to top it all off here I am paying 50 dollars a month for the membership and [...] Its has nothing to do with it being dirty since I deep clean it every time I use it… I have orders that I have to get out and I am losing money everyday because this thing wont work! I am so upset I cant even begin to explain it. I was so excited and thought it was just bumps and figuring it out but it seems like I have to fix or trouble shoot it after every use… Literally over it!

It is very hard to suggest a specific fix without specific information. I have had a pro for some years now and can look back to most problems as my lack of information. Many that were a machine issue are now gone. The Aura has benefitted about those issues but due to the diode vs CO2 changes new things have cropped up. They can also be fixed due to the cloud processing without you even knowing.

Image 19: u/Electronic_Disk5844 avatar

Miserable_Land_9004

•2y ago• Edited 1y ago

DO NOT get Aura god please do not. I have had it for 2 years. My identity was stolen literally last week and Aura did absolutely nothing to stop it. If you want a software that actually works, look elsewhere. Also, it will randomly block different processes on your computer and you won't understand why until you uninstall the app. I'm pissed at them right now, because you can't promise identity theft protection and proceed to allow very basic and easy identity theft to occur. It almost feels like a scam. [...] I think this is a case where a company is "trying" to be an all in one place for all of your security and identity needs and are excelling in parts and failing miserably in others.

8/15/24 purchased a 1yr subscription to Aura after my 4th identity theft incident this year. My internet connection became so unstable, constant error 404, after their Aura app loaded on my PC. I tried to reach customer service and was cutoff.

8/19/24 cancelled my future subscription and uninstalled their loaded software. Please don't make my mistake in believing Aura will provide a Quality Customer Experience, look elsewhere.

results.title: Aura Review 2026: I Tested It So You Don't Have To - YouTube
results.content: or ExpressVPN, you probably won't switch. It's fast enough for normal browsing, but I wouldn't rely on it for anything missionritical. The antivirus software is honestly the weakest part of the package. It catches basic threats, but if you're on Windows, you're probably better off sticking with Windows Defender or upgrading to something like Bit Defender. It's not bad. It's just not bestin-class. The password manager is actually pretty solid. It's not as featurerich as something like one password, but it does the basics well and integrates nicely with the rest of the Aura ecosystem. If you're not already using a password manager, this could be the nudge you need to start. Spam call protection is one of those features that's hard to measure, [music] but I have noticed fewer rooc calls [...] shrink over time. They're already handling takedowns from over 130 different broker sites, and that list just keeps on growing. Our team just noticed it went up again. And finally, we've got identity theft insurance, the break glass in case of emergency feature. Aura gives you up to $1 million in coverage with the option to upgrade to $5 million. Hopefully, you'll never need it, but if the worst happens and your identity does get stolen, you won't be stuck paying thousands out of pocket to repair the damage. Let's talk about all the extra stuff Aura throws in because this is where opinions vary. The VPN is decent, but nothing special. It works fine for basic privacy protection, but if you're already using something like NordVPN or ExpressVPN, you probably won't switch. It's fast enough [...] bureau credit monitoring and Aura starts looking pretty reasonable. We'll throw the latest prices on the screen, but just note that those are lower with the link [music] below. Let me walk you through the core features that actually protect your identity because this is where Aura either succeeds or fails. Credit monitoring is the biggest deal for most people. And here's why. If someone's tried to open a credit card, take out a loan, or even get a mortgage in your name, that attempt will show up on one of three credit bureaus. Aura covers all three: Experian, Equifax, and TransUnion. You'd think that would be standard, right? Shockingly, a lot of competitors only monitor one bureau. When I tested Aura, I got alerts within minutes of a credit inquiry. Not hours, not days, minutes. That

results.title: Aura Identity Theft Protection Review 2026 | Security.org
results.content:
Was Aura’s antivirus software the most powerful we’ve ever tested? No. Was their VPN the fastest? Also no. Did either of these come with the bells and whistles we saw when we reviewed NordVPN or put TotalAV to the test? Nope. We’re not faulting Aura for any of that though. We give credit where credit is due, and we appreciate Aura for putting together a service that conveniently includes most if not all features you need to protect your identity.

## The Drawbacks of Using Aura [...] In our opinion, these lackluster “side services” aren’t a deal breaker. They provide basic functionality and that’s okay. If you are looking for a comprehensive VPN or a top-notch antivirus program, you may want to consider a more bare-bones identity theft protection service so you’re not wasting money on features you won’t use.

Already Have Antivirus or a VPN You Trust?

LifeLock bundles Norton 360 — one of the top-rated antivirus platforms — into its identity protection plans. If device security is a priority, it’s worth comparing.

Product Logo for LifeLock

SecureScore™  9.8

View Plans  Links to LifeLock

## Video Review [...] #### What We Don't Like

 Limited budget-friendly options: Although Aura offers several different pricing packages, there aren't any basic tiers of service, meaning you can't purchase a lighter version for less money. The entry-level Individual plan starts at $9 per month when billed annually.
 Difficult to navigate: Aura offers lots of features, especially in the digital security space. It includes a VPN, antivirus software, parental control software and more. Some of those require different apps, making Aura harder to manage than other identity protection services.

#### Bottom Line
